story.text: As an AI re-homing stranded work, I need the owner-gesture index (loop/owner.py) to land as an atom on the log, because it was built and pushed (PR #510) but never announced, so the off-log gate refuses it as invisible work. The index answers 'bdo needs to decide X -- what is the gesture surface?' by folding each owner-decision KIND to the one opener pen that surfaces it on GitHub, and REFUSING any CLI-at-owner route -- the structural enforcement of the no-offload hard rule, so a session that cannot find the surface can never fall back to handing bdo a command.

briefing.headline: the owner-gesture index: a read-only fold mapping each owner-decision kind to its one opener pen, refusing the CLI-at-owner route, landable under epic.owner-harness
briefing.value: loop/owner.py is a pure read-only fold (the gaps/parity grain) that maps each kind of owner decision (arc-confirm, realness, rung, policy, issue) to the ONE opener pen that surfaces it as a GitHub gesture, and refuses a row that smells like a CLI-at-owner route (an `--by bdo` confirm command dressed as an opener). It makes the no-offload hard rule findable: a session asking 'what is bdo's gesture surface for X?' gets the pen, never a command to hand him. Rows whose opener does not resolve are flagged no-surface as real gaps rather than papered over.
briefing.why_now: The implementation was built and pushed on PR #510 but never carried an atom, so the off-log gate refuses the branch as invisible work. This atom announces it so it can enter the pipeline and land.
briefing.if_accepted: The owner-gesture index becomes a real surface on the log; sessions resolve bdo's gesture surfaces through one fold and can never legitimise handing him a CLI line.
briefing.if_rejected: loop/owner.py stays stranded off-log; the no-offload rule keeps no findable index and sessions keep re-deriving the surface map.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: owner.py is a pure read-only fold -- no gate logic, no write path, no truth moved. Reverting is deleting one read-only module and its test.
briefing.mechanism: loop/owner.py: a stdlib read-only fold over the opener pens on disk; validate() is the §10 teeth -- a row whose opener does not resolve to a real pen is a ghost, and a CLI-at-owner route is refused (tests/test_owner.py proves the check is non-vacuous). CLI ends done|report.
